semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all twenty-four assigned source notes and their limitations, three valid and five rejecting context-admission fixtures, two valid and nine rejecting resolver/certificate routes, one bounded four-project graph certificate and eleven rejecting mutations, QCSA's exact bounded identity, address, certificate, migration, cost, active-question, and support-state findings, all thirty-one theorem declarations grouped under six manifest targets, every manifest atom, every prose candidate, source crosswalk, test plan, summary, and handoff. The sweep made the static, versioned, consumer- and purpose-relative request-to-materialization contract the distinct owner; separated durable memory, dynamic transactions, verification adequacy, claim support, effects, rights, release, and resource authority; required exact actual-packet identity, source and field lineage, transformations, omissions, frontier, lifecycle, authority, rights, model-use observation, and complete denominators; and preserved zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Purpose-bound static request-to-packet materializations between durable source objects and actual model- or worker-visible context, including admitted, denied, faulted, stale, revoked, tainted, lossy, inadequate, unused, and residualized attempts.
scope.environment: Twenty-four reviewed assigned sources; three valid and five rejecting admission/adequacy fixtures; two valid and nine rejecting resolver/certificate routes; one bounded four-project certificate and eleven rejecting mutations; a deterministic 60-case, 13-system, three-seed QCSA corpus with 2,340 predictions and one zero-model vertical trace; thirty-one finite Lean declarations; and a future preregistered natural multi-model, multi-backend campaign. No deployed resolver, natural source-to-packet campaign, independently observed model use, production context effect, or useful advantage is represented.
scope.model: A versioned relation over frozen requests, durable identities and plural addresses, snapshots, candidate representations, actual emitted packets, source and field lineage, transformations, omissions, loss, provenance, taint, authority, rights, use contracts, selection and omitted frontier, adequacy states, leases, expiry, revocation, costs, faults, use observations, outcomes, and residuals; current implementations are schemas, record validators, synthetic routes, and finite theorems rather than a deployed context ABI.
scope.authority: Intent, Compilation, and Planning own accepted purpose and demand; durable memory owns objects and lifecycle; Context Transactions owns dynamic commits, mounts, isolation, taint, invalidation, deletion, and rollback; the ABI may materialize, deny, ask, abstain, refresh, narrow, broaden, or fault a static request; Verification owns adequacy; Runtime and Security own jobs, capabilities, approval, and effects; Claim Ledgers own support; Rights and Publication own use and release; Resource Economics owns cost. ABI admission grants no authority and moves no support or release state.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; source, snapshot, address atlas, representation, policy, authority, rights, evaluator, dependency, environment, consumer, target use, lease, generation, and revocation changes expire affected certificates and receipts.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest contract; twenty-four reviewed source mappings; public context, certificate, packet, adequacy, transaction-reference, and bounded graph-snapshot schemas; admission, resolver, certificate, graph, QCSA, and vertical-trace records; six Lean proof targets; future natural source sets, actual packets, independent obligation and use labels, complete costs, reproductions, and cross-model and cross-backend transfers.

assumptions: Natural public-safe source sets can expose material identity, omission, staleness, authority, poisoning, compression, and frontier failures without requiring prohibited disclosure.
Independent-enough adjudicators can label source obligations, actual packet contents, model use, task outcomes, delayed residuals, and uncertainty without sharing all resolver blind spots.

Full context, direct and graph RAG, hierarchical summary, prompt compression, persistent memory, adaptive retrieval, citation systems, OS-style tiering, human curation, governed ABI, and no-context routes can be matched on models, sources, tools, authority, budgets, repair opportunities, and horizons.

Actual model-visible bytes or content, ordering, placement, truncation, cache state, downstream use, and outcome contribution can be instrumented without treating attention or citation as ground truth.

Every request, candidate, retrieval, transformation, certificate, validation, cache event, retry, intervention, failure, cost, discard, use observation, and residual can be retained closely enough to expose denominator changes.
Purpose, sufficiency, acceptable loss, authority, rights, privacy, evaluator acceptance, use, support, and release remain explicit normative premises rather than inferred facts.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Modern long-context models, direct and graph RAG, hierarchical retrieval, prompt compression, persistent memory, OS-style tiering, citations, adaptive retrieval, ordinary provenance, access control, and human curation already provide adequate context management at lower complexity. A separate ABI adds brittle metadata, false precision, verifier and privacy overhead, and certificate theater while still being unable to prove source truth, semantic adequacy, actual model use, useful contribution, safety, or transfer.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A reachable ordinary or adversarial trace silently changes or loses frozen identity, source or field lineage, transformation, omission, frontier, lifecycle, authority, right, use, adequacy, actual packet, cost, fault, or residual state; a required field lacks an exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence; a forged or stale certificate is admitted; model presence is mistaken for use; or a strong simpler comparator dominates the preregistered joint fidelity, usefulness, safety, latency, and total-cost frontier.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze current long-context, RAG, graph retrieval, hierarchical summary, compression, persistent-memory, adaptive-retrieval, citation, OS-tiering, provenance, access-control, and human-curation comparators without importing source-reported quality.
Justify consumer and purpose, source obligations, acceptable loss, authority, rights, privacy, use, sufficiency, abstention, residual, evaluator, retention, and release boundaries.

Specify typed source, request, candidate, packet, certificate, lifecycle, fault, use, and outcome relations with countermodels and trace correspondence beyond trusted Booleans and assumption-restating predicates.

Run real durable stores, retrievers, compressors, packet emitters, certificate validators, cache and expiry paths, actual model calls, use instrumentation, fault handling, and effect gates end to end.

Measure source and field fidelity, obligation recall and precision, adequacy, model use, task utility, unsafe output, abstention, missed help, stale and poisoning resistance, latency, resources, human work, recovery, and total governance cost jointly.

Ablate stable identity, field lineage, actual-packet validation, omitted frontier, lifecycle, authority and rights, typed faults, use observation, and complete denominators under matched packets and histories.
Repeat across models, modalities, languages, durable stores, retrieval and compression backends, organizations, jurisdictions, threats, horizons, and time with independent implementations.

contrary_evidence: Long-context models, RAG, graph retrieval, summaries, compression, persistent memory, citations, adaptive retrieval, OS-style tiering, provenance, access control, and human curation already implement substantial pieces without a distinct ABI.

The current admission harness has only three valid fixtures and five expected-invalid controls; the resolver harness has two valid routes and nine rejecting controls; both are authored record checks rather than natural context outcomes.

The graph certificate covers one bounded four-project record and eleven rejecting mutations; it does not establish open-world completeness, retrieval quality, source truth, model use, or useful contribution.

The thirty-one theorem declarations include five operational or authority assumptions/projections and twenty-six consequences of finite Boolean or enumerated routes; they do not prove a real resolver, truthful certificates, semantic adequacy, model use, usefulness, safety, or transfer.
Full QCSA and its best selected baseline tied at 1.000 task accuracy, while QCSA used 1.913386 times the operations and failed matched-advantage and resource gates.

QCSA without plural facets scored 0.916667 object accuracy; without identity indirection it scored 0.900000 object accuracy and only 0.400000 migration compatibility versus 1.000000 for the full system, but these are bounded non-core synthetic transitions.
Removing active questions did not reduce object or task accuracy on the frozen QCSA corpus, so active-question value is refuted for that workload.
QCSA records zero Virtual Context ABI core support effect, and its thirteen-stage vertical trace uses no model calls and only one reversible temporary-file effect.

Certificates, packet metadata, independent validation, instrumentation, retention, revalidation, and complete ledgers can increase latency, compute, storage, privacy exposure, human work, abstention, and maintenance cost without improving outcomes.
known_confounds: Human-authored source obligations, packet labels, use labels, and outcomes can be incomplete, outcome-informed, culturally narrow, or non-unique.
Governed ABI routes may receive more tokens, retrievals, graph depth, tools, context, validation, repair, or human work than baselines.
Resolver, packet generator, certificate validator, model, use observer, and outcome evaluator may share model families, training data, prompts, code, infrastructure, organizations, or incentives.
Presence, attention proxies, citation, copying, or self-report can be mistaken for actual use or causal contribution.
Schema validity, digest parity, address confidence, graph position, round-trip reconstruction, and finite theorem coverage can be mistaken for fidelity or adequacy.
Rejected candidates, missing sources, failed retrievals, blocked packets, retries, cache events, stale results, abstentions, costs, and residuals can be removed from denominators.
Short horizons can miss stale-cache use, delayed poisoning, descendant invalidation, rights violations, harmful reliance, and recovery incompleteness.
Non-release can reduce observed harm while increasing false denial, missed help, latency, and opportunity cost.
Template-generated QCSA cases can reward the same identity and address rules used to generate labels and conceal ambiguity or distribution shift.
acceptance_criteria: Every request, source, field, candidate, transformation, omission, frontier, certificate, packet byte or content identity, lifecycle transition, authority, right, use, cache event, fault, cost, model-use observation, outcome, and residual field has an exact producer, consumer, owner, valid case, rejecting mutation, and observed consequence.

A preregistered natural campaign meets every joint fidelity, adequacy, usefulness, unsafe-output, abstention, missed-help, poisoning, staleness, cost, causal, reproduction, and transfer gate against strong matched comparators.

Each claimed mechanism has a predicted causal signature that disappears under matched ablation and cannot be explained by extra resources, hidden human curation, weak baselines, correlated evaluators, presence-use confusion, denominator changes, template leakage, or non-release.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact consumer, purpose, source set, snapshot, representation, resolver, packet, model, task, evaluator, environment, authority, rights, tools, budget, threat, horizon, outcomes, costs, and residuals.
Separate resolver conformance, source freshness, representation fidelity, admission, verification adequacy, model use, outcome contribution, usefulness, safety, support, and release.
Unknown source truth, open-world completeness, model use, causal contribution, delayed outcome, privacy effect, reproduction, or transfer remains residual, blocked, narrowed, or refuted.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable ordinary or adversarial trace violates it or a required field lacks an exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence.

Refute a mechanism benefit when matched ablation preserves the joint outcome, the mechanism worsens the frontier, or its effect is explained by extra resources, curation, weak baselines, correlated evaluators, denominator changes, template leakage, or non-release.

Refute faithful, adequate, useful, safer, efficient, reproducible, or transferable ABI language when actual packets drift, certificates lie, model use is absent, costs dominate, a simpler comparator wins, or reproduction and transfer fail.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise memory, transaction, retrieval, compilation, verification, runtime, security, artifact, evidence, resource, rights, or release owner supersedes the atom and every consumer migrates with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze and execute the natural context campaign with full-context, direct and graph RAG, hierarchical summary, compression, persistent memory, adaptive retrieval, citation, OS-tiering, human-curated, governed ABI, and no-context routes; identical models, sources, tools, authority, budgets, and horizons; independent source, packet, use, and outcome labels; stale, poison, omission, authority, cache, and frontier attacks; complete attempts and costs; causal ablations; clean reproduction; and transfer to a second model and memory backend.

non_claims: A typed schema, complete certificate, signature, digest, address confidence, bounded graph, round trip, green admission, finite route theorem, or citation does not establish source truth, semantic adequacy, model use, usefulness, safety, or support.

The current three valid admission fixtures, five admission controls, two valid resolver routes, nine resolver controls, one bounded graph certificate, eleven graph mutations, 2,340 synthetic predictions, one zero-model vertical trace, and thirty-one theorem declarations establish only their exact record checks, finite consequences, bounded mixed outcomes, and no-promotion boundaries.
No durable-memory state, transaction, job, authority, effect, claim support, deployment, publication, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owner and accepted transition.

virtual-context-abi.core.strongest_counterclaim: Existing retrieval, provenance, access-control, and prompt assembly components already cover this boundary with less machinery.
virtual-context-abi.core.falsifier: The contract has no unique exercised consumer or does not expose a material failure missed by simpler matched routes.
acceptance_criteria: Demonstrate independently checked source-to-packet fidelity, model use, useful outcome contribution, complete cost, causal mechanism signatures, reproduction, and transfer.

virtual-context-abi.problem.001.strongest_counterclaim: A sufficiently capable long-context model can infer this metadata from prompt content.
virtual-context-abi.problem.001.falsifier: Matched natural trials show anonymous prompt text loses no material identity, lifecycle, authority, omission, use, or outcome information.
acceptance_criteria: Predeclare natural cases where boundary information changes a correct admission, fault, use, or outcome decision.

virtual-context-abi.insufficiency.001.strongest_counterclaim: A composed modern RAG or memory stack already provides the same conformance contract.
virtual-context-abi.insufficiency.001.falsifier: A matched comparator supplies every required field, owner, rejection, use observation, and consequence without ABI-specific machinery.
acceptance_criteria: Implement and test strongest composed comparators, not labels or straw baselines.

virtual-context-abi.minimum.001.strongest_counterclaim: The existing validators and theorems are already sufficient proof of the chapter.
virtual-context-abi.minimum.001.falsifier: The prose promotes natural fidelity, model use, usefulness, safety, or transfer from current bounded artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Keep all promotions blocked until the declared natural campaign and evidence transition complete.

virtual-context-abi.beyond_sota.001.strongest_counterclaim: The challenge is too broad to be falsifiable and will reward governance overhead.
virtual-context-abi.beyond_sota.001.falsifier: It lacks matched comparators, joint gates, causal signatures, complete costs, or terminal narrowing and refutation rules.
acceptance_criteria: Freeze workloads, baselines, metrics, gates, ablations, evaluator separation, reproduction, and transfer before outcome access.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.002.strongest_counterclaim: Modern embeddings and graph identifiers are stable enough for practical context.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.002.falsifier: No matched natural collision, migration, readdressing, or cache case changes a material outcome.
acceptance_criteria: Test name, embedding, node, route, representation, packet, and support-state collisions independently.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.003.falsifier: Field-level lineage adds no detectable fidelity, fault, rights, or repair benefit over source links.
acceptance_criteria: Score preserved, transformed, split, merged, omitted, rejected, and residualized fields against independent labels.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.004.strongest_counterclaim: Logging omitted candidates is costly and rarely changes decisions.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.004.falsifier: Frontier disclosure never changes adequacy, abstention, error, attack detection, or cost estimates in matched natural trials.
acceptance_criteria: Inject hidden relevant, conflicting, poisoned, and authority-blocked candidates beyond declared bounds.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.005.strongest_counterclaim: Safe transformations can inherit ordinary downstream use by convention.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.005.falsifier: A derived packet gains undeclared authority, rights, purpose, support, or release scope.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise transformations against least-authority and least-rights policies with denying controls.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.007.strongest_counterclaim: Signed certificates and content digests are reliable enough.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.007.falsifier: Any prose or route treats declaration integrity as source-to-packet truth or adequacy.
acceptance_criteria: Use independent source and packet inspection plus forged, stale, partial, and self-consistent false certificates.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.008.strongest_counterclaim: Separate states add bureaucracy without changing decisions.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.008.falsifier: Collapsing the states does not change calibration, faults, outcomes, or support transitions under matched cases.
acceptance_criteria: Construct crossed cases for every state pair and observe downstream consequences.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.009.strongest_counterclaim: Best-effort retrieval is more useful than blocking.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.009.falsifier: Typed mandatory faults do not improve safety or correctness and cause unacceptable missed help.
acceptance_criteria: Measure useful throughput, unsafe use, missed help, latency, and residual burden jointly.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.011.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary cache TTLs and retriever refreshes are adequate.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.011.falsifier: Material-change receipts add no stale-state resistance over matched cache policies.
acceptance_criteria: Inject source, policy, atlas, authority, rights, evaluator, consumer, and use changes across live caches.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.012.strongest_counterclaim: Integrated memory agents can safely read and write through one abstraction.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.012.falsifier: No ordinary or adversarial trace turns packet materialization into unintended durable mutation, deletion, training, or effect authority.
acceptance_criteria: Test explicit read-only boundaries and transaction-owner handoffs with hostile packet content.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.013.strongest_counterclaim: The requested and selected packet is an adequate proxy for delivery.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.013.falsifier: Order, placement, truncation, byte identity, or cache differences never change use or outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Instrument exact consumer-visible content and compare it with requests and selection logs.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.014.strongest_counterclaim: Attention, citations, or copying adequately measure use.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.014.falsifier: Independent causal probes find no material gap among availability, placement, attention, reliance, and outcome contribution.
acceptance_criteria: Use counterfactual packet interventions and independent outcome observers rather than self-report alone.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.015.strongest_counterclaim: Success-path telemetry is sufficient for engineering decisions.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.015.falsifier: Reintroducing hidden failures, retries, cache events, human work, discards, and residuals does not alter any estimate.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile all emitted attempts and total resource, privacy, recovery, and opportunity costs.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.016.strongest_counterclaim: Recall, citations, latency, and token savings are adequate context quality proxies.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.016.falsifier: Every proxy remains calibrated to fidelity, usefulness, safety, and total cost under natural shift and attack.
acceptance_criteria: Report proxy and target outcomes jointly with disagreement and failure cases.

virtual-context-abi.invariant.017.strongest_counterclaim: Extensive schemas, fixtures, and formal checks justify broad confidence.
virtual-context-abi.invariant.017.falsifier: Any bounded artifact is cited as natural or transferable context-performance proof.
acceptance_criteria: Label exact populations and require separate natural reproduction and transfer evidence.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.002.strongest_counterclaim: Semantic addresses are accurate enough for practical identity.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.002.falsifier: Adversarial collisions and migrations produce no material wrong-object or wrong-version use.
acceptance_criteria: Test names, embeddings, paths, graph nodes, routes, and physical locations against durable identity.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.003.strongest_counterclaim: TTL and ordinary invalidation prevent meaningful stale use.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.003.falsifier: No stale, revoked, migrated, rolled-back, or policy-mismatched handle reaches a consumer.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise generation, lease, epoch, digest, policy, and revalidation failures concurrently.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.004.strongest_counterclaim: Modern compressors preserve task-relevant information reliably.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.004.falsifier: Matched natural adversarial summaries lose no material qualifier, negation, quantity, time, authority, citation, or contradiction.
acceptance_criteria: Score field and obligation fidelity beside utility, tokens, latency, and downstream reconstruction cost.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.007.strongest_counterclaim: Cryptographic integrity and schema completeness are sufficient operational assurance.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.007.falsifier: Independent adversarial validation finds no truthful-looking false certificate that changes admission or use.
acceptance_criteria: Test forged, stale, incomplete, self-consistent false, and wrong-source certificates.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.008.strongest_counterclaim: Bounded graph retrieval need not disclose unreachable material.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.008.falsifier: Omitted-frontier disclosure never changes adequacy, uncertainty, attack detection, or outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Place material evidence and conflicts just outside node, edge, depth, and time limits.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.009.strongest_counterclaim: Fresh relevant permitted context is normally adequate.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.009.falsifier: Admitted-but-inadequate cases never alter claims, decisions, or outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Cross conformance with fidelity, adequacy, use, usefulness, safety, and support independently.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.010.strongest_counterclaim: Context in the window is usually used by capable models.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.010.falsifier: Counterfactual interventions find no gap between available, placed, attended, relied-on, and outcome-relevant content.
acceptance_criteria: Instrument actual packets and use causal perturbations across position and load.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.012.strongest_counterclaim: Retriever ranking and source filtering already manage poisoning.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.012.falsifier: Matched attacks never induce wrong selection, silent conflict loss, unsafe reliance, or cost-dominated abstention.
acceptance_criteria: Inject source, graph, summary, cache, certificate, and semantic-address attacks with complete denominators.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.013.strongest_counterclaim: Standard cache keys and invalidation are adequate.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.013.falsifier: No cache path bypasses source, snapshot, policy, authority, rights, generation, expiry, or revocation checks.
acceptance_criteria: Trace cold, warm, shared, migrated, rolled-back, and adversarial cache paths.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.014.strongest_counterclaim: Unified memory-context abstractions are simpler and safe.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.014.falsifier: Materialization never creates unintended write, merge, deletion, training, or effect authority.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise owner boundaries under hostile content and concurrent transactions.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.016.strongest_counterclaim: Rejected and failed routes do not matter to delivered context quality.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.016.falsifier: Restoring every attempt, failure, intervention, discard, and residual leaves all estimates unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile request-to-outcome attempt ledgers with cost and opportunity denominators.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.017.strongest_counterclaim: Metadata overhead is negligible relative to better context control.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.017.falsifier: Matched natural trials show joint usefulness and safety gains without unacceptable total cost.
acceptance_criteria: Measure tokens, compute, storage, retrieval, validation, human work, privacy, latency, recovery, and opportunity cost.

virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.018.strongest_counterclaim: One synthetic identity corpus predicts general resolver portability.
virtual-context-abi.failure_mode.018.falsifier: Independent reproduction transfers across a second model and memory backend without material fidelity, usefulness, safety, or cost loss.
acceptance_criteria: Predeclare cross-model, cross-backend, cross-language, cross-modality, and temporal transfer gates.

virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-abi-opera.strongest_counterclaim: The declaration assumes address and version validity and concludes resolution, proving no resolver.
virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-abi-opera.falsifier: It is cited as deployed resolution or semantic identity evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Bind typed store states, executable resolution, collisions, migrations, and observed packets to the theorem.

virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-abi-failu.strongest_counterclaim: The declaration encodes the desired mandatory-miss policy rather than proving implementation behavior or benefit.
virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-abi-failu.falsifier: A real mandatory miss emits best effort or prose cites the theorem as useful fault evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Refine executable resolver traces to typed fault outcomes and measure missed-help and safety consequences.

virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-abi-conte.strongest_counterclaim: Trusted Booleans and branch priority prove only the authored policy.
virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-abi-conte.falsifier: A malformed or unsafe real packet reaches admission, or the theorem is called general adequacy proof.
acceptance_criteria: Bind typed requests, actual packets, independent validators, countermodels, and downstream observations.

virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-certifica.strongest_counterclaim: The declaration assumes source bindings and contracts exist and proves no truth or fidelity.
virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-certifica.falsifier: It is cited as source-to-packet correctness evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Bind certificate fields to independently inspected sources, transformations, and actual packet fields.

virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-certifica.strongest_counterclaim: The declaration restates that derived authority may not exceed source authority.
virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-certifica.falsifier: A transformation widens real authority or rights despite satisfying the model.
acceptance_criteria: Refine concrete authority and rights lattices through executable transformations and denying cases.

virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-certifica.strongest_counterclaim: Finite enumerated branches cannot establish truthful certificates, lifecycle enforcement, or useful context.
virtual-context-abi.formal.vcm-certifica.falsifier: A forged, stale, revoked, tainted, or scope-violating real certificate reaches use, or prose universalizes the route theorem.
acceptance_criteria: Bind actual certificate producers, stores, packets, validators, revocation and deletion events, use observations, and countermodels.

virtual-context-abi.prose.06e5a4f1b5e4.rationale: Reader-facing question fragment about the representation boundary.

virtual-context-abi.prose.b7cd27e25699.rationale: Sentence fragment completed by the surrounding distinction.

virtual-context-abi.prose.9066a2740bb0.rationale: Restates actual selection and omission disclosure.

virtual-context-abi.prose.25b6b21b2cec.rationale: Bounded statement about the OS-style comparator family.

virtual-context-abi.prose.f9e075f32903.rationale: Opening fragment of the distinct core owner.

virtual-context-abi.prose.fa3fe1d38fa3.rationale: Introduces the natural evidence still required.

virtual-context-abi.prose.d400abcffada.rationale: States the bounded QCSA mechanism inheritance.

virtual-context-abi.prose.53796805f500.rationale: Restates intent and planning owner boundary.

virtual-context-abi.prose.c93afc8cd255.rationale: Restates citations and packets cannot promote support.

virtual-context-abi.prose.33d677ed6e3d.rationale: Restates no authority or rights widening.

virtual-context-abi.prose.b11aff99bbce.rationale: Restates stale and materially changed packet non-inheritance.

virtual-context-abi.prose.0193fb11bf33.rationale: Restates a fault's downstream blocking consequence.

virtual-context-abi.prose.aae366d2eca2.rationale: Restates the exact current implementation non-claims.

virtual-context-abi.prose.d89bed1dd24d.rationale: Begins the next honest natural minimum.

virtual-context-abi.prose.2d6cd56793a2.rationale: Continues independent source, packet, use, and outcome labeling requirements.

virtual-context-abi.prose.fdc965ef68e7.rationale: Restates the mature ABI packet and ledger contract.

virtual-context-abi.prose.4a613173d67a.rationale: Line-wrapped continuation of the reachable-model limitation; it adds no independently promotable claim.

non_claims: Completing the semantic sweep does not promote the chapter core or convert schemas, authored fixtures, QCSA's bounded synthetic records, or finite theorems into natural context evidence.

QCSA's identity, plural-address, certificate, and migration findings remain bounded non-core mechanisms; its accuracy tie, operation-cost failure, active-question refutation, and zero core support effect remain intact.
No durable memory, transaction, authority, effect, support state, release, publication, license, AGI, or ASI claim changes through this review.
